pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh which explicitly configures this path and the Natural Earth download script.
Verified by reading setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which target and clean this specific file path.
Shape type 5 corresponds to Polygon in the ESRI Shapefile specification, which is the mathematically expected geometry for Voronoi/Thiessen diagrams.
The downloaded input dataset has exactly 243 points, which mathematically yields 243 Voronoi polygons. 200 is a valid lower bound.
The downloaded input dataset produces exactly 243 polygons. 280 is a valid upper bound.

Generating Voronoi polygons directly from the 243 input points using Geopandas yields a .shp file of ~40.6 KB. The claimed 50,000 byte minimum is contradicted by baseline shapefile generation, though specific gvSIG algorithm overhead might vary.

privileged_info_summary: The input dataset is the real public domain Natural Earth 1:110m Populated Places shapefile, which contains exactly 243 unique point features. A mathematically correct Voronoi tessellation on these points yields exactly 243 polygons (falling within the expected 200-280 range) with an ESRI shapefile geometry type of 5 (Polygon). Independent generation produced a shapefile of ~40.6 KB, indicating the claimed minimum file size of 50,000 bytes may be incorrect depending on the specific algorithm implementation.
